0.vision: To create an integrated civilization-scale stack that merges high-frequency propulsion control (LightDrive) with advanced cryptographic state inversion (Chronoplast) via a real-time capability-based microkernel (LoomOS).
0.architecture: A distributed hybrid architecture comprising a Rust-based Real-Time Operating System (LoomOS) utilizing a lock-free Ring Buffer (LoomBus) for agent communication, interfaced with a Python-driven mathematical reconstruction engine for algebraic inversion of cryptographic states.

techStack.frontend: React-based dashboard with WebGL for 3D Moire-Encryption visualization and real-time telemetry.
techStack.backend: Rust (LoomOS Kernel), Python 3.12 (NumPy/SciPy for Linke-Inversion), and C++20 for high-frequency GaN amplifier control.
techStack.database: PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB extension for Long-Term Memory (LTM) and append-only event logging.
techStack.infra: Bare-metal edge nodes for RTOS operations, integrated with FPGA (Xilinx Artix-7) for nanosecond-precision timing.

0.databaseSchema: Tables: agents (id, capability_token, status), ltm_logs (timestamp, sequence_id, vector_h, vector_n, vector_g, checksum), telemetry (id, force_output, hf_phase, temperature_c), passphrases (word, hash_target, entropy_level).
apiEndpoints: POST /api/v1/drive/force - Set target Newtons for LightDrive
STREAM /ws/v1/telemetry - Real-time propulsion and inversion data
0.securityStrategy: Granular capability-based security (POLA) using bit-masked 64-bit tokens for hardware access. All state transitions validated via SHA-256 checksums before persistence in LTM. Hardened GaN-Gate isolation for RF components.
0.scalingPlan: Horizontal scaling of reconstruction workers using Gitter-Matrix (LLL) reduction distribution; vertical scaling of LoomOS threads via hardware affinity on multi-core ARM/RISC-V processors.

1.folderStructure: root/├── loom-os (Rust)/│   ├── src/│   │   ├── bus/ (LoomBus)│   │   ├── capability/ (POLA tokens)│   │   └── kernel/ (Scheduler)├── chronoplast (Python)/│   ├── engine/ (Linke-Inversion)│   ├── solvers/ (Meatgrinder)│   └── api/ (FastAPI)├── propulsion (C++20)/│   ├── gan_driver/│   ├── pll_control/│   └── fpga_bridge/├── dashboard (React)/│   ├── src/│   │   ├── moire_vis/ (WebGL)│   │   └── hooks/ (Telemetry)└── infra/│   ├── postgres/ (TimescaleDB)│   └── bitstream/ (Xilinx Artix-7)
1.stateManagement: Distributed state machine with LTM persistence in TimescaleDB; Local state via lock-free ring buffers in Rust; Frontend state via Redux Toolkit with RTK Query for telemetry ingestion.
1.cachingStrategy: L1: Local SRAM for RTOS critical paths; L2: Redis for transient cross-node telemetry; L3: Memoization of algebraic inversion matrices in Python memory pool.
1.rateLimiting: Hardware-level interrupt coalescing for GaN control; Leaky Bucket algorithm at API Gateway (10,000 req/sec per capability token).
1.errorBoundaryStrategy: Rust Panic-to-Reset for kernel threads; Circuit Breaker pattern for reconstruction workers; Hardware watchdog timers for GaN-Gate isolation; React Error Boundaries for 3D visualization failures.

2.backendBoilerplate: use crossbeam::queue::SegQueue; use std::sync::Arc; pub struct LoomBus { queue: Arc<SegQueue<BusMessage>>, reactor: tokio::runtime::Runtime } impl LoomBus { pub fn new() -> Self { Self { queue: Arc::new(SegQueue::new()), reactor: tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread().enable_all().build().unwrap() } } pub fn dispatch(&self, msg: BusMessage) { self.queue.push(msg); } } #[tokio::main] async fn main() { let bus = LoomBus::new(); loop { if let Some(cmd) = bus.queue.pop() { process_capability_command(cmd).await; } } }
frontendComponents: MoireVisualizer: WebGL-based component for real-time interference pattern rendering of propulsion flux.
TelemetryDashboard: A grid-based layout using D3.js and Three.js to visualize high-frequency sensor data.
CapabilityManager: UI module for managing POLA token permissions and session-based hardware access.
PropulsionController: Low-latency interface for sending SPSC commands to the GaN driver via the Rust kernel.
2.authImplementation: Capability-Object Security Model (POLA). Authentication initiates via OAuth2 to obtain a master JWT. The Loom OS kernel then derives scoped Capability Tokens (CIDs) with bitmask-encoded permissions (e.g., GAN_WRITE, TELEMETRY_READ). These tokens are verified at the LoomBus reactor level using a lock-free lookup table.
2.models: CREATE TABLE telemetry (time TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, device_id UUID NOT NULL, flux_vector VECTOR(3), gate_temperature FLOAT, bus_voltage FLOAT); SELECT create_hypertable('telemetry', 'time'); CREATE TABLE capability_tokens (id UUID PRIMARY KEY, bitmask BIGINT, expiration TIMESTAMP, owner_id UUID);
2.apiClientConfig: import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react'; export const telemetryApi = createApi({ reducerPath: 'telemetryApi', baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({ baseUrl: '/api/v1', prepareHeaders: (headers) => { headers.set('X-Capability-Token', localStorage.getItem('pola_token')); return headers; } }), endpoints: (builder) => ({ getLiveStream: builder.query({ query: () => 'propulsion/stream' }) }) });
2.loggingMiddleware: Implementation uses a custom Rust log-facade that redirects all thread-local panics to an SPSC ring-buffer. The Chronoplast Python API utilizes a FastAPI middleware to inject correlation IDs into the 'X-Loom-Request-ID' header, ensuring trace continuity from the WebGL dashboard through the link-inversion solvers to the hardware driver.

3.injectionPrevention: Enforce parameterized queries using SQLx for all TimescaleDB interactions; strict schema validation for LoomBus messages using Protobuf to prevent malformed propulsion commands; hardware-level bounds checking on GaN driver SPSC inputs to prevent register-clobbering injections.
3.cspPolicy: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' wss://loom-kernel:50051; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; worker-src 'self' blob:; manifest-src 'self'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';
3.csrfImplementation: Utilization of the Capability-Object model which eschews ambient authority (cookies) in favor of the 'X-Capability-Token' header; supplemental HMAC-signed request-binding for GAN_WRITE operations to ensure command origin integrity.
3.advancedRateLimit: Token-bucket throttling implemented at the LoomBus reactor level; per-CID rate limiting for TELEMETRY_READ (capped at 500Hz) and hard-governed physical limits for GAN_WRITE (10ms minimum interval) to prevent hardware fatigue via rapid cycling.
3.encryptionStandard: In-transit: TLS 1.3 with mandatory Mutual TLS (mTLS) between the API, Reactor, and FPGA controller; At-rest: AES-256-GCM for TimescaleDB volumes and ChaCha20-Poly1305 for sensitive FPGA bitstream storage.
securityHeaders: Permissions-Policy: accelerometer=(), camera=(), geolocation=(), gyroscope=(), magnetometer=(), microphone=(), payment=(), usb=()
3.sanitizationLogic: Type-safe deserialization via Rust Serde with range-validation for gate_temperature and bus_voltage; floating-point normalization for flux_vector to prevent NaN-injection attacks in the MoireVisualizer WebGL shaders.
3.auditLogDesign: Immutable, append-only security ledger stored in a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE); records all CID issuance, bitmask escalations, and thread-local panic traces from the Rust log-facade with nanosecond-resolution hardware timestamps.

4.dockerConfig: Multi-stage Rust build using 'rust:1.75-bookworm' for compilation with SQLx-offline mode and 'gcr.io/distroless/cc-debian12' as the runtime base to minimize attack surface. Includes installation of ca-certificates for TLS 1.3/mTLS and strict non-root user execution (UID 1000).
4.k8sManifests: Deployment specs include 'Loom-Reactor' and 'API-Gateway' with sidecar containers for mTLS proxying (Envoy). Service objects define ClusterIP for internal Protobuf traffic and LoadBalancer with TLS termination for the WebGL frontend. NetworkPolicies restrict ingress to the TimescaleDB pod only from the Reactor namespace.
4.ciCdPipeline: GitHub Actions workflow: 1. Cargo clippy/fmt for linting; 2. Cargo test for unit/integration; 3. SQLx-data validation; 4. Kaniko image build with SHA-256 signing; 5. Trivy vulnerability scanning; 6. GitOps trigger for ArgoCD deployment to the production cluster.
4.terraformPlan: AWS Provider: Provisioning an EKS cluster with private subnets across 3 AZs. Managed RDS instance with TimescaleDB extension enabled. KMS keys for AES-256-GCM volume encryption. IAM roles for Service Accounts (IRSA) allowing TEE (Trusted Execution Environment) access for audit logs.
4.monitoringStack: Prometheus operator scraping Rust 'metrics' crate endpoints; Grafana dashboards visualizing 'gate_temperature' and 'flux_vector' via MoireVisualizer telemetry; Alertmanager integration for hardware-level panic traces and LoomBus reactor throttling events.
4.autoScalingRules: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) targeting 65% CPU utilization for API nodes. Custom Metrics API scaling for the Reactor based on 'LoomBus_SPSC_Queue_Depth'. Hard limit of 50 nodes to prevent budget exhaustion during telemetry spikes.
4.backupStrategy: Daily snapshots of TimescaleDB volumes using AWS Backup with cross-region replication. Continuous WAL (Write Ahead Log) archiving to S3 with 30-day retention. TEE security ledger streamed to immutable S3 Object Lock bucket for audit integrity.

5.unitTestSuite: Comprehensive Rust coverage using 'cargo test' targeting LoomBus SPSC logic, Protobuf serialization schemas, and SQLx-offline queries. Includes property-based testing with 'proptest' for gate_temperature boundary conditions and non-root UID 1000 file permission checks.
5.integrationTests: Validation of mTLS handshakes between API-Gateway and Loom-Reactor via Envoy sidecars. Integration testing of TimescaleDB migrations using testcontainers-rs and cross-service Protobuf RPC calls over internal ClusterIP including network policy ingress verification.
e2eScenarios: Simulate WebGL telemetry stream: Verify data flow from Gateway to Loom-Reactor, persistence in TimescaleDB hypertable, and real-time MoireVisualizer update in Grafana.
Trigger LoomBus_SPSC_Queue_Depth spike: Validate HPA scaling to 50 nodes, hardware-level panic trace alerts, and subsequent TEE audit log entry in S3 Object Lock bucket.
5.loadTestingParams: Stress test targeting 65% CPU HPA threshold; ramp-up to 10k concurrent WebGL sessions. Focus on LoomBus saturation points, Protobuf serialization throughput, and TLS 1.3 handshake latency under maximum cluster expansion.
5.perfOptimizations: Implementation of Link-Time Optimization (LTO) and codegen-units=1 in release profile. Utilization of Jemalloc for efficient memory management and SIMD instructions for processing flux_vector telemetry batches.
5.dbOptimization: Partitioning TimescaleDB hypertables by 'time' and 'device_id'. Implementation of Continuous Aggregates for 1-minute telemetry rollups and BRIN indexing for high-density flux_vector data to optimize query performance.
5.bundleOptimization: Multi-stage Docker caching strategies to reduce build times, binary stripping using 'strip' to minimize distroless image size, and Brotli compression for WebAssembly assets served to the WebGL frontend.
5.latencyImprovements: Migration to gRPC for internal service communication to reduce overhead compared to REST. Kernel-level TCP tuning for EKS nodes and implementation of mTLS session resumption to decrease TLS handshake overhead.

6.apiDocumentation: Comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1 specification exposed via the API-Gateway for RESTful ingestion, complemented by a private Buf Schema Registry for internal gRPC service definitions and Protobuf message contracts.
adminDashboardFeatures: Real-time LoomBus SPSC queue depth and saturation heatmaps
Flux_vector telemetry rollup configuration and hypertable retention management
TEE audit log viewer with S3 Object Lock integrity verification
HPA scaling threshold overrides and EKS node group status visualizer
MoireVisualizer configuration studio for Grafana-integrated telemetry streams
6.analyticsStrategy: Real-time stream processing of flux_vector telemetry using TimescaleDB Continuous Aggregates for 1-minute rollups, coupled with BRIN-indexed historical analysis and Grafana-based performance visualization.
6.featureFlags: Centralized dynamic configuration plane for toggling SIMD-optimized processing routines, managing canary rollouts of gRPC service updates, and runtime adjustment of LoomBus buffer capacities.
6.multiTenancy: Logical isolation implemented via non-root UID 1000 container permissions, TimescaleDB device_id partitioning, and strict mTLS-enforced namespace segmentation using EKS Network Policies.
6.whiteLabeling: Customizable WebGL frontend assets with themeable MoireVisualizer components and support for client-specific Brotli-compressed WASM modules for branded telemetry experiences.
6.operationsRunbook: Standard procedures for mTLS certificate rotation via Envoy, automated TimescaleDB hypertable partitioning maintenance, and EKS cluster expansion protocols triggered by LoomBus saturation metrics.
6.troubleshootingGuide: Diagnostic trees for resolving gRPC latency spikes, identifying hardware-level panic causes via TEE audit traces, and recovering from LoomBus SPSC buffer overflows during maximum 10k session load.
6.executiveSummary: A high-performance, Rust-native telemetry and data processing platform designed for extreme scale. Leveraging LoomBus for low-latency internal messaging and TimescaleDB for high-density persistence, the system delivers millisecond-level responsiveness for 10k concurrent WebGL sessions. Hardened with mTLS and TEE-based auditing, it provides an enterprise-grade solution for real-time flux_vector analysis and visualization.
